attributes.action: Make it mandatory by law to give interview outcome to all candidates.
attributes.additional_details: Candidates invest significant time preparing for and attending job interviews, often alongside work or personal commitments often including preparing presentations and assessments. Regardless of career stage, it is only fair they are informed of the outcome. Failing to do so creates unnecessary uncertainty and can be discouraging undermining confidence in recruitment processes.
attributes.background: Make it a legal requirement for employers to provide an outcome to any candidate who has attended at least one formal interview, in person or online, within a defined timeframe, such as 14 days, excluding informal recruiter conversations.

rejection.details: You may be interested in this petition, which calls for similar action: "Legal requirement to respond to job applicants after interview" https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/753619
